title: Factually incorrect response
prompt: Explain why the sky appears blue.
candidate_response: The sky is blue because oceans reflect their color onto the atmosphere and this is always true.
notes: Contains a planted factual error and absolute claim.

title: Logically flawed argument
prompt: Should all software teams enforce code review? Explain your reasoning.
candidate_response: Every team must enforce code review because it is optional in fast-moving teams, therefore mandatory reviews are unnecessary but still required.

title: Technically answers but misses point
prompt: How can a startup reduce cloud costs in the next 30 days without hurting reliability?
candidate_response: Cloud providers have many services. Startups should learn AWS, Azure, and GCP documentation and understand cloud history.
notes: Surface-level response that fails the practical requirement.

title: High-quality response
prompt: How can a startup reduce cloud costs in the next 30 days without hurting reliability?
candidate_response: In 30 days, start by rightsizing over-provisioned compute based on 7-day usage metrics, adding autoscaling floors to protect reliability, and deleting unattached storage snapshots. Next, turn on commitment planning for stable workloads and move bursty jobs to spot/preemptible capacity with fallback nodes. Finally, enforce cost alerts and weekly reliability checks (latency, error rate, and incident counts) to confirm savings do not degrade service quality.
notes: Balanced, complete answer with practical steps and reliability safeguards.
